texto: Historians of technology have asked why no industrial revolution developed in antiquity. The simple answer seems to be that there was no need, that contemporary modes of production and the slave-based economy of the day satisfactorily maintained the status quo. The capitalist idea of profit as a desirable end to pursue was completely foreign to the contemporary mentality. So, too, was the idea that technology on a large scale could or should be harnessed to those ends. An industrial revolution was literally unthinkable in antiquity.
explicacao: Orientação direta de James E. McClellan sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “Historians of technology have asked why no industrial revolution developed in antiquity. The simp…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, history, industrial-revolution, útil em momentos de termino e industrial-revolution.
